item_specifications.special_feature: Airline Approved, Breathable Mesh, Foldable Design, Washable Pad, Anti-Scratch Mesh, Safety Leash Inside, Side Pocket, Deluxe Zippers, Padded Shoulder Strap

about_item: Perfect for Small Pets – Recommended for pets up to 15 lbs, 15 inches in length, and 9 inches in height — ideal for small dogs, cats, and puppies.
Premium Safety Features – Anti-scratch mesh, sturdy ABS hooks, and an interior safety leash keep your pet secure and comfortable.
Airline-Approved Design – Fits under most airplane seats for stress-free travel (check with airline before flying).
Convenient & Comfortable – Side pocket holds treats and toys; padded shoulder strap and handles make carrying easy.
Easy to Clean & Store – Includes a removable, washable mat and folds flat for compact storage when not in use.

authors_reviews.title: Perfect carry bag for small animals.
authors_reviews.text: Very nice bag for small animals.
authors_reviews.author: Jon Cardwell

authors_reviews.title: Very Helpful
authors_reviews.text: The way that I use this carrier might offend some people, and I apologize in advance for that. But sometimes we find ourselves in situations where we need to do what we need to do. And I will say that this travel carrier has been a life saver for me.I absolutely HATE dogs in grocery stores, as I'm sure most people do. The entitlement of seeing someone's little dust mop dog riding in the seat of the shopping cart with their fluffy blanket and, sometimes even a toy, is truly offensive. I don't want to be buying produce with dog hair on it. And even more obnoxious is putting little service vests on these dogs who are clearly just pampered pets. We have all seen these dogs. And I absolutely do not want to be one of those people. Unfortunately, my dog rides with me for work and some days I really and truly NEED to stop at the grocery store to grab and item or two for that night's dinner. Driving home to drop my little guy off and then going back out is just a major amount of time that I don't have. Sooooooo... one day in a real pinch, I decided to try this carrier because I had left it in the car with the intention of testing it out for the review.Firstly, the carrier folds completely flat and sits behind my driver seat without being in the way of anything. It doesn't have any real support, so opening it up is a bit of a nuisance, but if you have a well behaved dog, it's really not a problem. Thankfully, mine sat politely in his car set and waited. Simply pull it open and zip the one end to give it a bit of stability. I put my dog in (with his little fluffy blanket) and zipped the other end closed. He was safely bundled in and wouldn't get cold in the store.The carrier was a bit less sturdy than I would have liked, but when you factor in that the trade off is that it folds flat and can be conveniently stored, I think it's really a fair trade. There's plenty of ventilation, but it's also closed in enough to make the dog feel secure and safe the same way their crate will. He wobbled some as I carried him and that bothered me, so i prefer to park close to the cart corral in the future so he can be immediately on a more stable surface. He was quite content riding in the carrier and almost no one realized he was there because I simply placed him quietly down into the shopping cart.The thing that I loved most was that the few people who commented about him being in the store were being very positive. I heard things like "If you have to bring a dog in here, that's the way to do it." and "What a nice way to keep your dog." The cashier even told me that she wished more people would be considerate like that rather than lie about the dog being a service animal. It made me feel good about my decision. I still don't like having a dog in a grocery store, but this carrier made the experience manageable and I didn't feel like a complete creep (only about a half a creep).Would I want to travel with him in this carrier? I'm not really sure. I really like it better if it can be sitting on top of something to add more stability to the bottom. So, carrying him in it for the length of an airport would be concerning. But overall, I think it's a really handy piece of equipment that every dog owner would appreciate and find use for.
authors_reviews.author: Jeff & Mindy

authors_reviews.title: A bit flimsy at bottom, easy to put cat in
authors_reviews.text: Pros:• Lightweight.• Straps are easy to hook and unhook.• Multiple zipper openings: top, front and sides. Easy to drop cats into carrier. Back is covered with canvas. No mesh.• Compact.• Comfortably fit a 10lb cat with room to spare. Maybe even two 10lb cats but no room.Cons:• Mesh isn’t heavy duty. Better for calm cats.• Mesh on most sides makes the carrier too open for scared cats. Needs a towel to cover the carrier.• Bottom support is flimsy. Soft lining is thin and require additional bedding inside to make it more comfortable.Overall it’s a good soft carrier for easy cats. Don’t use with stressed or aggressive cats and ensure there’s additionally support at the bottom of you got a tubby cat.
authors_reviews.author: B. Bui
